summary: REQ-FEED-033 forbidden-identifier grep covers `src/lib/block-editor/`, but ui-block-editor REQ-BE-027 already owns `src/lib/block-editor/` and uses a different identifier set. Two parallel, non-identical regex audits over the same directory will drift over time without an explicit ownership/delegation contract.
context: ui-block-editor REQ-BE-027 (lines 720-727 of `.vcsdd/features/ui-block-editor/specs/behavioral-spec.md`) audits `src/lib/block-editor/` for `EditorIpcAdapter|EditorViewState|EditorAction|EditorCommand|EditingSessionStateDto|EditingSessionStatus|subscribeToState`. REQ-FEED-033 audits the same directory for `EditorPanel|editorStateChannel|tauriEditorAdapter|editorReducer|editorPredicates|EditorViewState|EditorAction|EditorCommand|EditorIpcAdapter`. The intersection is `EditorViewState`, `EditorAction`, `EditorCommand`, `EditorIpcAdapter` (4 identifiers). REQ-FEED-033 adds `EditorPanel/editorStateChannel/tauriEditorAdapter/editorReducer/editorPredicates`. REQ-BE-027 adds `EditingSessionStateDto/EditingSessionStatus/subscribeToState`. There is no statement of which feature is authoritative for the union, so a future drift (e.g., ui-block-editor adds a new forbidden identifier) will not propagate.
recommendedFix: Add to REQ-FEED-033 an explicit delegation note: 'For src/lib/block-editor/ identifiers, the authoritative forbidden-list is maintained by ui-block-editor REQ-BE-027. REQ-FEED-033 only adds the EditorPane-specific identifiers (EditorPanel, editorStateChannel, tauriEditorAdapter, editorReducer, editorPredicates) which are not part of REQ-BE-027 because they were never moved into block-editor.' Or restrict REQ-FEED-033 grep to `src/lib/feed/` and `src/routes/+page.svelte` only and let REQ-BE-027 handle `src/lib/block-editor/`.
